description: Ontology rebuilt from v4 manifest constructs (2026-03-17). Relationships fuzzy-matched from v1 (threshold=0.20). Unresolved legacy relationships in legacy_unresolved.

Perceptions of rights influence views on problems faced by children.

Teacher quality impacts overall perception of school quality.
Research indicates that higher interest in science correlates with increased consumption of science-related media (e.g., media about science).
Cultural exposure and knowledge reinforce cultural identity and patriotism.
Family support is linked to higher motivation and positive attitudes towards education.
Greater environmental knowledge is associated with more environmentally friendly behaviors.
Perceptions of children's rights influence awareness and attitudes toward issues faced by children.

External influences like religion and government policies can shape family structures over time.

Higher trust may lead to increased political engagement.
Perceived corruption negatively correlates with trust in institutions.

Perceptions of justice influence opinions on how well the justice system functions.

Higher national pride may relate to stronger values and potential conflicts between values.
Public emotional climate can reflect underlying value conflicts.
Attitudes towards democracy influence satisfaction with the democratic system.

Migration experience can shape attitudes towards foreigners.
Positive attitudes towards foreigners may correlate with openness to different religions.
Religious attitudes can influence feelings of national pride.
Attitudes towards migration may affect overall life satisfaction.
Perceptions of societal diversity may influence attitudes toward others.
Cultural heritage may impact individual life satisfaction.
Trust in institutions correlates with overall life satisfaction.

Perception of indigenous identity can affect experiences of discrimination and racism.
Perceptions and stereotypes shape attitudes towards indigenous people.
Discrimination impacts socioeconomic opportunities for indigenous populations.
Socioeconomic status influences perceived advantages or disadvantages of being indigenous.
Perceptions of age influence attitudes towards older individuals.

Negative attitudes may lead to discriminatory behaviors.

Valores y normas influyen en las percepciones sociales sobre justicia, división y comportamiento social.
El nivel de satisfacción y bienestar puede influir en las aspiraciones futuras.
El control sobre aspectos de la vida refleja valores de autonomía y autodeterminación.

Trust in government can affect perceptions of economic fairness and comparisons.
Perceptions of causes may shape overall economic satisfaction.

Self-reported health influences perceived physical limitations.

metadata._read_this_first: This ontology encodes SES-mediated covariation between survey constructs in Mexican public opinion data (los_mex, 26 surveys, ~1200 respondents each). BEFORE querying any edge or node, understand two constraints: (1) Every bridge edge gamma(A,B) measures ONLY the covariation between A and B that is mediated through the 4 SES dimensions below — it captures nothing else. (2) The 4 SES dimensions [escol, Tam_loc, sexo, edad] are ALL equal. Do not treat escol as the canonical SES variable and the others as controls. Gender and age structure Mexican attitudes as strongly as education and location. See bridge_cia_note for full list of what gamma cannot capture. See docs/ONTOLOGY_GUIDE.md for complete reference.
metadata.ses_vars_note: All 4 are SES dimensions with equal standing: escol=education, Tam_loc=town size/urban-rural, sexo=gender, edad=age/cohort. None is a 'demographic control' subordinate to the others. The DR bridge conditions on all 4 simultaneously. gamma(A,B) measures covariation mediated by the full 4D sociodemographic position.
metadata.bridge_cia_note: CRITICAL LIMITATION: bridge gamma(A,B) measures ONLY the covariation between constructs A and B that is mediated through the 4 SES variables above. Under the Conditional Independence Assumption (CIA), once you condition on SES cell membership, A and B are assumed independent. gamma CANNOT capture: (1) direct A->B causal links independent of SES, (2) confounding by variables outside the 4-var SES set, (3) reverse causation B->A, (4) interaction effects between SES dims. A near-zero bridge gamma means SES does NOT co-drive A and B monotonically -- it does NOT mean A and B are unrelated in general.
metadata.gamma_sign_convention: gamma is signed. Positive gamma(A,B): SES co-elevates both constructs. Negative gamma(A,B): SES raises one while depressing the other. Prediction chain uses the SIGNED product: loading_gamma_A x gamma(A,B) x loading_gamma_B. loading_gamma of an L0 item is negative when reverse-coded (item up = less of concept). Double negatives cancel correctly. Use absolute value only for reporting magnitude.
metadata.ses_fingerprint_note: Each L1 construct carries a 4D SES fingerprint vector [rho_escol, rho_Tam_loc, rho_sexo, rho_edad]. ses_magnitude = RMS of all 4 rho values: overall SES stratification intensity. ses_sign = sign(rho_escol) is kept as a fast single-number summary but should not be treated as the definitive SES direction — it is only one of 4 equal dimensions. BRIDGE SIGN PREDICTION: dot(fingerprint_A, fingerprint_B) predicts the sign of gamma(A,B) at 99.4% accuracy across 984 significant bridges. This is the correct alignment measure: two constructs whose SES profiles point in the same 4D direction are co-elevated by the same sociodemographic position -> gamma > 0.

_patch_history.reason: RC fix after scale_audit_v1 — fingerprint_cos + gamma signs corrected
